summary: yt-api-dlp: lookalike name shipping a copy of yt-dlp with no attribution
details: Package name `yt-api-dlp` is a single-token insertion away from the widely-used `yt-dlp` package, and the tarball contains a near-verbatim copy of the yt-dlp source tree (version 2026.06.09, internal ORIGIN='yt-dlp/yt-dlp') republished under a different name with blank project metadata (empty description, empty author, empty readme). No install-time lifecycle hooks are declared, and traced import-time behavior mirrors upstream yt-dlp aside from an added `print("The Unlicense")` in __init__.py; the auto-updater still points at the genuine yt-dlp GitHub releases with SHA256 verification. No exfiltration, dropper, credential theft, silent-relay, or backdoor is present. Installer harm is limited to running an unattributed, potentially stale copy of yt-dlp under a confusable name — a name-confusion concern that warrants human review to confirm intent (typo-lure vs. benign fork) before publishing an advisory.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
